title: Polymer clay flower hair clip
subtitle: Layered clay petals assembled into a flower and mounted on a clip base
excerpt: A polymer clay flower hair clip is built from individually shaped petals, assembled around a centre, baked hard at 130°C, and glued onto a metal alligator clip or barrette base. The petals are shaped by pinching and pressing before the clay is baked, so the flower is fully formed before it goes in the oven. No kiln is needed.
sourceNotes: Polymer clay floral jewellery technique. Technique derived from first principles.

content.text: flowers are built in two rings of petals plus a centre. Each petal is pinched to shape, assembled with the others on a flat clay disc, then
in the oven. The assembly bakes as one piece. The hair clip base is attached with strong adhesive after baking.

attrs.body: Alligator clips and barrette bases are available from craft suppliers. The flat back of the cured flower sits directly on the clip. Use a two-part epoxy adhesive for a strong bond. Allow the adhesive to cure overnight before wearing.
attrs.title: Clip bases

items.name: Polymer clay, main colour and centre colour
Alligator clip or barrette base and strong adhesive

content.text: Condition the main colour clay. Roll a ball 2 cm in diameter and flatten it into a disc 3 cm across and 4 mm thick. This is the hidden base onto which the petals attach.

Pinch off a pea-sized piece of clay. Roll it into a ball, then press and flatten it into an oval. Pinch the tip slightly to a point. Cup the petal slightly by pressing the centre with a fingertip. Make five petals for the outer ring and four slightly smaller petals for the inner ring.

Press the five outer petals around the edge of the base disc, evenly spaced, with the tips pointing outward. Press the base of each petal firmly against the disc. Add the four inner petals overlapping the gaps between the outer petals, with the tips pointing slightly upward. Use the needle tool to press each petal base into the disc and smooth the join.

Roll a small ball of the centre colour clay about 8 mm in diameter. Press it into the centre of the flower, covering the inner petal bases. Texture the centre by pressing the end of the needle tool repeatedly into its surface to create a dotted pattern.

Place the flower face-up on baking paper on a dedicated tray. Bake at 130°C for 25 minutes. Cool completely. Apply adhesive to the flat back of the flower and press the clip base into position. Allow the adhesive to cure fully before wearing.
Use two shades of the same colour for the outer and inner rings of petals for a more graduated effect.
Mount the flower on a brooch pin back instead of a hair clip.

attrs.intro: Problems when making the flower hair clip.

items.fix: Press each petal base with the needle tool handle, applying firm pressure for three seconds. Avoid picking the flower up by the petals before baking.
items.cause: Petal base not pressed firmly enough into the disc.
items.symptom: Petals fall away from the disc when the flower is handled before baking

items.fix: Roll the clay into a log and cut equal slices with the craft knife before rolling each into a ball. Equal starting volumes give equal petal sizes.
items.cause: No system for cutting equal-sized pieces of clay before shaping.
items.symptom: Petals are uneven in size across the flower

attrs.heading: Common problems
